name: Caponata I
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Eggplant, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
Extra virgin olive oil (more, if necessary)
Medium Yellow or red onion, peeled and sliced in half
Ripe paste tomatoes such as Roma
3 Bay leaves (or more; fresh leaves should be finely chopped after removing the petiole and midrib)
Red wine vinegar, or to taste
1/4 cup Fresh basil leaves sliced thinly at the last minute
recipeInstructions:
1. Place eggplant cubes in a colander and sprinkle generously with salt. Let stand for at least half an hour, rinse well under cold water, drain, and pat dry with paper towels.
2. Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in a large heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Gradually add the eggplant, stirring constantly. Add more oil as needed to prevent sticking.
3. Cook until all the eggplant is in the pan. Gradually add garlic, onion, and celery. Keep the heat high as you cook until hot through but not browned.
4. Stir in tomatoes, bay leaves, vinegar, capers, and olives. Mix well and lower the heat to simmer. Cook for about 15-20 minutes or until the eggplant is tender and the flavors are well developed.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and additional vinegar as needed. Add basil leaves, nuts, and parsley before serving. Serve hot, at room temperature, or chilled.
Variation: In the last few minutes of cooking, add chopped anchovies, chunks of fish or shellfish, ham, Italian sausage, or other cooked meat.
